In [64]:
# Time Series notebook -- setup & load MBA handoff
import pickle, warnings, os
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')
plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-whitegrid')

with open('mba_handoff.pkl', 'rb') as _f:
    _h = pickle.load(_f)

df_units          = _h['df_units']
df_used_market    = _h['df_used_market']
name_map          = _h['name_map']
segmentation_data = _h['segmentation_data']
df_core           = _h.get('df_core',   pd.DataFrame())
df_whale          = _h.get('df_whale',  pd.DataFrame())
lrfm              = _h.get('lrfm',      None)
best_k_core       = _h.get('best_k_core', None)
success_stories   = _h['success_stories']
sow_final         = _h['sow_final']

print('MBA handoff loaded.')
print(f'  df_units          {df_units.shape}')
print(f'  df_core           {df_core.shape}   Stage2 clusters: {best_k_core}')
print(f'  df_whale          {df_whale.shape}')
print(f'  lrfm loaded       {lrfm is not None}')
print(f'  success_stories   {len(success_stories)} rows')
print(f'  sow_final         {len(sow_final)} rows')

MBA handoff loaded.
  df_units          (7539, 46)
  df_core           (1371, 11)   Stage2 clusters: 7
  df_whale          (73, 9)
  lrfm loaded       True
  success_stories   76 rows
  sow_final         10 rows

TIME SERIES ANALYSIS: using the BCR MACROTRENDS

STL Decomposition — Trend, Seasonality, and Residuals¶

In [68]:
from statsmodels.tsa.seasonal import STL

# Build a clean monthly time series from the used machinery dataset
_df_stl = df_units.copy() # Corrected: Use the pre-loaded df_units DataFrame
_df_stl['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(
    _df_stl['ANO'].astype(str) + '-' + _df_stl['MES'].astype(str) + '-01'
)
ts_volume = _df_stl.groupby('Date').size().asfreq('MS').fillna(0)

# STL decomposition (robust=True reduces influence of outliers)
stl = STL(ts_volume, period=12, robust=True)
stl_result = stl.fit()

fig, axes = plt.subplots(4, 1, figsize=(14, 10), sharex=True)
for ax, data, label, color in zip(
        axes,
        [stl_result.observed, stl_result.trend, stl_result.seasonal, stl_result.resid],
        ['Observed', 'Trend', 'Seasonal', 'Residual'],
        ['black', 'steelblue', 'green', 'red']):
    ax.plot(data, color=color, lw=1.5)
    ax.set_ylabel(label)
    if label == 'Residual':
        ax.axhline(0, color='black', lw=0.8, ls='--')
axes[0].set_title('STL Decomposition — Monthly Used Machinery Import Volume (2015–2026)')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('5_stl_decomposition.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

# Average seasonal pattern by calendar month
_monthly_seasonal = (
    pd.Series(stl_result.seasonal.values, index=stl_result.seasonal.index)
    .groupby(lambda d: d.month).mean()
)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))
_monthly_seasonal.plot(kind='bar', color=['green' if v > 0 else 'red' for v in _monthly_seasonal], edgecolor='black')
plt.xticks(range(12),
           ['Jan','Feb','Mar','Apr','May','Jun','Jul','Aug','Sep','Oct','Nov','Dec'],
           rotation=45)
plt.title('Average Seasonal Effect by Calendar Month')
plt.ylabel('Seasonal component (units)')
plt.axhline(0, color='black', lw=0.8)
plt.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('5_seasonal_effect_calendar_month.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

print("\nPeak import months (positive seasonal effect):")
print(_monthly_seasonal[_monthly_seasonal > 0].sort_values(ascending=False))

Peak import months (positive seasonal effect):
Date
11    9.010357
12    4.916775
8     4.594217
10    4.335846
1     4.232119
4     3.264141
7     1.620146

In [70]:
# ── Build Monthly Panel: Import_Volume + PBI_Construccion + PEN_USD ───────
df_gdp = pd.read_csv('cleaned_gdp_peru.csv')
df_gdp['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(df_gdp['Date'])

_ts = df_units.copy()
_ts['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(_ts['ANO'].astype(str) + '-' + _ts['MES'].astype(str) + '-01')
_ts = _ts[_ts['Date'] <= pd.to_datetime('2025-12-31')]

monthly_vol = _ts.groupby('Date').size().rename('Import_Volume')
monthly_vol.index = pd.to_datetime(monthly_vol.index)

df_gdp_monthly = df_gdp[['Date', 'PBI_Construccion']].set_index('Date').sort_index()

# ── Load BCR exchange rate: PEN per USD (nominal, monthly average) ─────────
_MES = {'Ene':1,'Feb':2,'Mar':3,'Abr':4,'May':5,'Jun':6,
         'Jul':7,'Ago':8,'Sep':9,'Oct':10,'Nov':11,'Dic':12}

def _parse_bcr(s):
    """Convert 'Ene95' / 'Mar26' to a pd.Timestamp (handles 2-digit years)."""
    yr = int(s[3:])
    yr = yr + 1900 if yr >= 90 else yr + 2000
    return pd.Timestamp(yr, _MES[s[:3]], 1)

_fx_raw = pd.read_csv(
    r'/Users/fabriziocontreras/Desktop/KUL/Masters/Thesis/data/bcr-macrotrends/pen-usd-nominal.csv',
    skiprows=2, header=None, names=['period', 'PEN_USD']
).dropna(subset=['period'])
_fx_raw['Date']   = _fx_raw['period'].apply(_parse_bcr)
_fx_raw['PEN_USD'] = pd.to_numeric(_fx_raw['PEN_USD'], errors='coerce')
df_fx = _fx_raw.set_index('Date')[['PEN_USD']].sort_index()

# ── Merge into ts_data ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ts_data = (
    monthly_vol.to_frame()
    .join(df_gdp_monthly, how='inner')
    .join(df_fx,          how='left')
    .asfreq('MS')
    .dropna()
)
ts_data = ts_data[ts_data.index <= pd.to_datetime('2025-12-31')]

print(f'ts_data: {ts_data.shape}  ({ts_data.index[0].date()} → {ts_data.index[-1].date()})')
print(ts_data.tail(20))

ts_data: (132, 3)  (2015-01-01 → 2025-12-01)
            Import_Volume  PBI_Construccion   PEN_USD
Date                                                 
2024-05-01             57        197.646544  3.731727
2024-06-01             55        197.999031  3.786705

2024-08-01             43        225.159371  3.741850
2024-09-01             56        227.774879  3.768962

2024-12-01            109        315.195699  3.735537

2025-02-01             78        190.022446  3.696850

2025-04-01             96        212.044868  3.700030
2025-05-01            117        210.362133  3.659914

2025-07-01            120        232.600023  3.556945

2025-11-01            181        247.335666  3.372630
2025-12-01            182        353.050702  3.366863
In [72]:
# ── Multicollinearity Check: Variance Inflation Factor (VIF) ─────────────
# VIF measures how much the variance of a regression coefficient is inflated
# due to linear dependence with other predictors.
# Rule of thumb: VIF < 5 acceptable; 5–10 moderate concern; > 10 severe.
# Applied to the exogenous regressors before including them in SARIMAX/VECM.

from statsmodels.stats.outliers_influence import variance_inflation_factor
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

_exog_cols = ['PBI_Construccion', 'PEN_USD']
_X = ts_data[_exog_cols].copy()

# Add constant (VIF is computed with an intercept)
_X_const = _X.assign(const=1.0)

_vif = pd.DataFrame({
    'Variable': _exog_cols,
    'VIF': [
        variance_inflation_factor(_X_const.values, i)
        for i in range(len(_exog_cols))
    ]
}).round(3)

print("=" * 44)
print("  Variance Inflation Factor — Exogenous Variables")
print("=" * 44)
display(
    _vif.style
    .format({'VIF': '{:.3f}'})
    .apply(lambda col: [
        'background-color: #d4edda' if v < 5 else
        'background-color: #fff3cd' if v < 10 else
        'background-color: #f8d7da'
        for v in col
    ], subset=['VIF'])
)

# Correlation matrix
_corr = _X.corr().round(3)
print("\n  Correlation matrix (PBI_Construccion vs PEN_USD):")
display(_corr)

# Interpretation
for _, row in _vif.iterrows():
    if row['VIF'] < 5:
        _interp = "acceptable — no multicollinearity concern"
    elif row['VIF'] < 10:
        _interp = "moderate — acknowledge as limitation"
    else:
        _interp = "SEVERE — consider differencing or dropping one variable"
    print(f"  {row['Variable']:<22}  VIF={row['VIF']:.3f}  {_interp}")

============================================
  Variance Inflation Factor — Exogenous Variables
============================================
  Variable VIF
0 PBI_Construccion 1.209
1 PEN_USD 1.209 
  Correlation matrix (PBI_Construccion vs PEN_USD):
PBI_Construccion PEN_USD
PBI_Construccion 1.000 0.416
PEN_USD 0.416 1.000 
  PBI_Construccion        VIF=1.209  acceptable — no multicollinearity concern
  PEN_USD                 VIF=1.209  acceptable — no multicollinearity concern

Stationarity Tests — ADF & KPSS (prerequisite for Johansen)¶

In [75]:
# ── Stationarity Tests: ADF and KPSS ──────────────────────────────────────
# Required before running Johansen cointegration: both series must be I(1).
# ADF  → H₀: unit root (non-stationary).  p < 0.05 → reject → stationary.
# KPSS → H₀: stationary.                  p < 0.05 → reject → non-stationary.
# Consistent signal for I(1): level is non-stationary; first difference is stationary.

from statsmodels.tsa.stattools import adfuller, kpss

def _stationarity_report(series, name):
    print(f"\n{'='*56}")
    print(f"  Series: {name}")
    print(f"{'='*56}")

    # ── Level ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    adf_stat, adf_p, _, _, adf_crit, _ = adfuller(series.dropna(), autolag='AIC')
    kpss_stat, kpss_p, _, kpss_crit    = kpss(series.dropna(), regression='c', nlags='auto')
    print(f"  Level")
    print(f"    ADF  stat={adf_stat:8.4f}  p={adf_p:.4f}  cv5%={adf_crit['5%']:.4f}"
          f"  → {'STATIONARY ✓' if adf_p < 0.05 else 'non-stationary ✗'}")
    print(f"    KPSS stat={kpss_stat:8.4f}  p={kpss_p:.4f}  cv5%={kpss_crit['5%']:.4f}"
          f"  → {'non-stationary ✗' if kpss_p < 0.05 else 'STATIONARY ✓'}")

    # ── First difference ────────────────────────────────────
    d = series.diff().dropna()
    adf_d, adf_pd, _, _, adf_crit_d, _ = adfuller(d, autolag='AIC')
    kpss_d, kpss_pd, _, kpss_crit_d    = kpss(d, regression='c', nlags='auto')
    print(f"  First difference")
    print(f"    ADF  stat={adf_d:8.4f}  p={adf_pd:.4f}  cv5%={adf_crit_d['5%']:.4f}"
          f"  → {'STATIONARY ✓' if adf_pd < 0.05 else 'non-stationary ✗'}")
    print(f"    KPSS stat={kpss_d:8.4f}  p={kpss_pd:.4f}  cv5%={kpss_crit_d['5%']:.4f}"
          f"  → {'non-stationary ✗' if kpss_pd < 0.05 else 'STATIONARY ✓'}")

    is_I1 = (adf_p >= 0.05) and (kpss_p < 0.05) and (adf_pd < 0.05) and (kpss_pd >= 0.05)
    print(f"  ⟹  Integration order: {'I(1) ✓  — eligible for Johansen test' if is_I1 else 'AMBIGUOUS — review carefully'}")

_stationarity_report(ts_data['Import_Volume'],    'Import_Volume')
_stationarity_report(ts_data['PBI_Construccion'], 'PBI_Construccion')

_stationarity_report(ts_data['PEN_USD'], 'PEN_USD (PEN per USD, nominal)')

========================================================
  Series: Import_Volume
========================================================
  Level
    ADF  stat= -0.4263  p=0.9056  cv5%=-2.8853  → non-stationary ✗
    KPSS stat=  1.1556  p=0.0100  cv5%=0.4630  → non-stationary ✗
  First difference
    ADF  stat= -3.0012  p=0.0348  cv5%=-2.8853  → STATIONARY ✓
    KPSS stat=  0.1707  p=0.1000  cv5%=0.4630  → STATIONARY ✓
  ⟹  Integration order: I(1) ✓  — eligible for Johansen test

    ADF  stat= -1.9854  p=0.2930  cv5%=-2.8839  → non-stationary ✗
    KPSS stat=  1.3661  p=0.0100  cv5%=0.4630  → non-stationary ✗
  First difference
    ADF  stat=-10.1787  p=0.0000  cv5%=-2.8840  → STATIONARY ✓
    KPSS stat=  0.3511  p=0.0982  cv5%=0.4630  → STATIONARY ✓
  ⟹  Integration order: I(1) ✓  — eligible for Johansen test

SARIMAX — Order Selection (AIC/BIC Grid Search) & Model Fit¶

In [78]:
# ── SARIMAX Order Selection: AIC/BIC Grid Search ──────────────────────────
# Grid: p,q ∈ {0,1,2}  d ∈ {0,1}  seasonal (P,D,Q,12) with P,D,Q ∈ {0,1}
# Training window: 2015–2024. Final model fitted on full ts_data (2015–2025).

from statsmodels.tsa.statespace.sarimax import SARIMAX
import itertools

_train_cut  = pd.to_datetime('2024-12-01')
_ts_train   = ts_data[ts_data.index <= _train_cut]
_endog_tr   = _ts_train['Import_Volume']
_exog_tr    = _ts_train[['PBI_Construccion', 'PEN_USD']]

_grid = list(itertools.product(range(3), range(2), range(3)))            # (p,d,q)
_seas = [(P, D, Q, 12) for P, D, Q in itertools.product(range(2), range(2), range(2))]

_best_aic, _best_order, _best_seas = np.inf, None, None
_table = []

for (p, d, q), s in itertools.product(_grid, _seas):
    try:
        _m = SARIMAX(_endog_tr, exog=_exog_tr,
                     order=(p, d, q), seasonal_order=s,
                     enforce_stationarity=False, enforce_invertibility=False)
        _r = _m.fit(disp=False)
        _table.append({'ARIMA': f'({p},{d},{q})', 'Seasonal': str(s),
                       'AIC': round(_r.aic, 2), 'BIC': round(_r.bic, 2)})
        if _r.aic < _best_aic:
            _best_aic, _best_order, _best_seas = _r.aic, (p, d, q), s
    except Exception:
        continue

_df_sel = pd.DataFrame(_table).sort_values('AIC')

# ── Best D=0 spec: always shown even if outside top-30 ───────────────────
_df_d0 = _df_sel[_df_sel['Seasonal'].str.contains(r'\(\d+, 0,')]  # D=0 rows
_best_d0 = _df_d0.iloc[0] if not _df_d0.empty else None

print("Top 30 SARIMAX specifications by AIC (training sample 2015–2024):")
print(_df_sel.head(30).to_string(index=False))
print(f"\n✅  AIC-selected : ARIMA{_best_order} × Seasonal{_best_seas}  AIC={_best_aic:.2f}")
if _best_d0 is not None:
    print(f"📌  Best D=0 spec: ARIMA{_best_d0['ARIMA']} × Seasonal{_best_d0['Seasonal']}  AIC={_best_d0['AIC']}  BIC={_best_d0['BIC']}")
else:
    print("⚠️  No D=0 specifications converged.")

# ── Researcher override: ARIMA(0,1,2) × Seasonal(0,0,1,12) ─────────────────
# The AIC-selected model (2,1,2)×(0,1,1,12) produces degenerate estimates on
# the full sample: all AR/MA parameters non-significant (p > 0.38), MA.S.L12
# std err = 1533, sigma² std err = 4.9×10⁵.  This signals over-parameterisation
# and near-perfect collinearity between the seasonal MA and the seasonal
# difference (D=1).  The parsimonious ARIMA(0,1,2)×(0,0,1,12) specification —
# one non-seasonal difference, two MA terms, one seasonal MA without seasonal
# differencing — is adopted following Sagaert et al. (2018), who recommend
# balancing fit and interpretability for medium-horizon B2B demand forecasts.
_best_order = (0, 1, 2)
_best_seas  = (0, 0, 1, 12)
print(f"\n🔧  Override → ARIMA{_best_order} × Seasonal{_best_seas}  "
      f"(Best D=0 AIC on training: {_best_d0['AIC'] if _best_d0 is not None else 'n/a'})")

# ── Fit on full ts_data (2015–2025) ─────────────────────────────────────────
sarimax_model = SARIMAX(ts_data['Import_Volume'],
                        exog=ts_data[['PBI_Construccion', 'PEN_USD']],
                        order=_best_order,
                        seasonal_order=_best_seas,
                        enforce_stationarity=False,
                        enforce_invertibility=False)
results = sarimax_model.fit(disp=False)
print("\n--- SARIMAX Model Summary ---")
print(results.summary())

# ── Forecast setup ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
last_pbi       = ts_data['PBI_Construccion'].iloc[-1]
last_fx        = ts_data['PEN_USD'].iloc[-1]           # random-walk assumption for FX forecast
future_periods = 30   # Jan 2026 → Jun 20328 (2,5 years × 12 months)
forecast_index = pd.date_range(ts_data.index[-1] + pd.offsets.MonthBegin(1),
                               periods=future_periods, freq='MS')
growth_rates   = {
    'Optimistic (+5% GDP Growth)':       0.05,
    'Base Case (+3% GDP Growth)':        0.03,
    'Pessimistic (-2% GDP Contraction)': -0.02,
}
print(f"\nForecast window: {forecast_index[0].date()} → {forecast_index[-1].date()}")

Top 30 SARIMAX specifications by AIC (training sample 2015–2024):

Forecast window: 2026-01-01 → 2028-06-01
In [79]:
# non-siignificant estimates. trying a more parsimoniues model
In [80]:
import pandas as pd
from statsmodels.tsa.statespace.sarimax import SARIMAX

# Define targets and regressors clearly from your dataset
endog_var = ts_data['Import_Volume']
exog_vars = ts_data[['PBI_Construccion', 'PEN_USD']]

# ── MODEL 1: ARIMA(0,1,2) x Seasonal(0,1,0,12) ───────────────────────────
# Removes the unstable seasonal MA term completely, relying only on seasonal differencing.
model_1 = SARIMAX(endog_var, exog=exog_vars,
                  order=(0, 1, 2),
                  seasonal_order=(0, 1, 0, 12),
                  enforce_stationarity=False,
                  enforce_invertibility=False)
results_1 = model_1.fit(disp=False)

# ── MODEL 2: ARIMA(0,1,2) x Seasonal(0,0,1,12) ───────────────────────────
# Removes seasonal differencing, allowing the seasonal MA term to estimate freely.
model_2 = SARIMAX(endog_var, exog=exog_vars,
                  order=(0, 1, 2),
                  seasonal_order=(0, 0, 1, 12),
                  enforce_stationarity=False,
                  enforce_invertibility=False)
results_2 = model_2.fit(disp=False)

# ── Print Comparative Summaries ──────────────────────────────────────────
print("=" * 80)
print(f"   [MODEL 1]: ARIMA(0,1,2)x(0,1,0,12) | AIC: {results_1.aic:.2f} | BIC: {results_1.bic:.2f}")
print("=" * 80)
print(results_1.summary())

print("\n\n" + "=" * 80)
print(f"   [MODEL 2]: ARIMA(0,1,2)x(0,0,1,12) | AIC: {results_2.aic:.2f} | BIC: {results_2.bic:.2f}")
print("=" * 80)
print(results_2.summary()) 


# ── Adopt Model 2 as the operational model for all downstream cells ─────
# User-selected specification: ARIMA(0,1,2) × Seasonal(0,0,1,12).
# This overrides _best_order / _best_seas from the grid-search cell so
# the Chancay and COVID structural break tests use the correct order.
results     = results_2
_best_order = (0, 1, 2)
_best_seas  = (0, 0, 1, 12)
print(f'\n✅  Operational model locked: ARIMA{_best_order} × Seasonal{_best_seas}')
print(f'    AIC = {results.aic:.2f} | BIC = {results.bic:.2f}')

Structural Break Test — COVID-19 Pandemic (Mar 2020)¶

In [85]:
# ── Structural Break Test: COVID-19 Pandemic (Mar 2020) ─────────────────
# Peru declared a national health emergency on 15 March 2020, imposing one
# of the world's strictest lockdowns — a strong candidate structural break
# in the import volume series.
#
# H₀: the COVID-19 shock had no significant level effect on monthly import
#     volume after conditioning on PBI_Construccion and PEN_USD.
#
# Method: re-fit ARIMA(0,1,2)×(0,0,1,12) with D_COVID (=1 from Mar-2020)
# as an additional exogenous dummy.  Two evidence pieces:
#   (a) t-test on the dummy coefficient  → direct significance
#   (b) ΔAIC (model with vs. without)   → whether break improves overall fit
# Power note: ~70 post-break months available — well-powered test.

covid_date = pd.to_datetime('2020-03-01')
_D_covid   = (ts_data.index >= covid_date).astype(int)

exog_with_covid             = ts_data[['PBI_Construccion', 'PEN_USD']].copy()
exog_with_covid['D_COVID']  = _D_covid

sarimax_covid = SARIMAX(ts_data['Import_Volume'],
                        exog=exog_with_covid,
                        order=_best_order,
                        seasonal_order=_best_seas,
                        enforce_stationarity=False,
                        enforce_invertibility=False)
results_covid = sarimax_covid.fit(disp=False)

_coef_c  = results_covid.params['D_COVID']
_se_c    = results_covid.bse['D_COVID']
_tstat_c = results_covid.tvalues['D_COVID']
_pval_c  = results_covid.pvalues['D_COVID']
_daic_c  = results.aic - results_covid.aic   # positive = dummy improves fit

print('=' * 60)
print('  Structural Break Test — COVID-19 Pandemic (Mar 2020)')
print('=' * 60)
print(f'  SARIMAX order used : ARIMA{_best_order} × Seasonal{_best_seas}')
print()
print(f'  AIC without dummy  : {results.aic:.2f}')
print(f'  AIC with dummy     : {results_covid.aic:.2f}')
print(f'  ΔAIC               : {_daic_c:+.2f}  '
      f'({"dummy improves fit ✓" if _daic_c > 2 else "negligible / no improvement"})')
print()
print(f'  D_COVID  coef = {_coef_c:+.4f}')
print(f'           s.e. = {_se_c:.4f}')
print(f'           t    = {_tstat_c:.4f}')
print(f'           p    = {_pval_c:.4f}')
print()
if _pval_c < 0.05:
    print('  ⟹  SIGNIFICANT (p < 0.05) — reject H₀.')
    print(f'     The COVID-19 shock is associated with a level shift of '
          f'{_coef_c:+.1f} units/month in import volume, after controlling for GDP and FX.')
else:
    print('  ⟹  NOT significant (p ≥ 0.05) — fail to reject H₀.')
    print('     No statistically detectable level break at α = 0.05.')
    print('     Note: STL decomposition captures the 2020 contraction in the trend')
    print('     component; the level shift may be absorbed by the differenced ARIMA structure.')
print()
print('  Full model summary (with dummy):')
print(results_covid.summary())

============================================================
  Structural Break Test — COVID-19 Pandemic (Mar 2020)
============================================================
  SARIMAX order used : ARIMA(0, 1, 2) × Seasonal(0, 0, 1, 12)

  AIC without dummy  : 1019.81
  AIC with dummy     : 1021.53
  ΔAIC               : -1.72  (negligible / no improvement)

  D_COVID  coef = +9.4823
           s.e. = 93.6414
           t    = 0.1013
           p    = 0.9193

  ⟹  NOT significant (p ≥ 0.05) — fail to reject H₀.
     No statistically detectable level break at α = 0.05.
     Note: STL decomposition captures the 2020 contraction in the trend
     component; the level shift may be absorbed by the differenced ARIMA structure.

Structural Break Test — Chancay Megaport (Nov 2024)¶

In [87]:
# ── Structural Break Test: Chancay Megaport (Nov 2024) ───────────────────
# Known break date → dummy variable approach, equivalent to Chow (1960) for
# a single known breakpoint in a regression context.
#
# H₀: the Chancay opening had no significant level effect on monthly import
#     volume after conditioning on PBI_Construccion and PEN_USD (the SARIMAX exogs).
#
# Method: re-fit the same SARIMAX order with D_Chancay (=1 from Nov-2024)
# added as an exogenous dummy.  Two evidence pieces:
#   (a) t-test on the dummy coefficient  → direct significance
#   (b) ΔAIC (model with vs. without)   → whether break improves overall fit
# Limitation: only ~14 months of post-break data exist at time of writing;
# low post-break sample reduces power (Type-II error risk if β is small).

chancay_date   = pd.to_datetime('2024-11-01')
_D             = (ts_data.index >= chancay_date).astype(int)

# Keep ALL original regressors so the comparison is valid (same spec).
exog_with_dummy              = ts_data[['PBI_Construccion', 'PEN_USD']].copy()
exog_with_dummy['D_Chancay'] = _D
sarimax_chow = SARIMAX(ts_data['Import_Volume'],
                       exog=exog_with_dummy,
                       order=_best_order,
                       seasonal_order=_best_seas,
                       enforce_stationarity=False,
                       enforce_invertibility=False)
results_chow = sarimax_chow.fit(disp=False)

# ── Results table ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
_coef  = results_chow.params['D_Chancay']
_se    = results_chow.bse['D_Chancay']
_tstat = results_chow.tvalues['D_Chancay']
_pval  = results_chow.pvalues['D_Chancay']
_daic  = results.aic - results_chow.aic   # positive = dummy improves fit

print("=" * 60)
print("  Structural Break Test — Chancay Megaport (Nov 2024)")
print("=" * 60)
print(f"  SARIMAX order used : ARIMA{_best_order} × Seasonal{_best_seas}")
print()
print(f"  AIC without dummy  : {results.aic:.2f}")
print(f"  AIC with dummy     : {results_chow.aic:.2f}")
print(f"  ΔAIC               : {_daic:+.2f}  "
      f"({'dummy improves fit ✓' if _daic > 2 else 'negligible / no improvement'})")
print()
print(f"  D_Chancay  coef = {_coef:+.4f}")
print(f"             s.e. = {_se:.4f}")
print(f"             t    = {_tstat:.4f}")
print(f"             p    = {_pval:.4f}")
print()
if _pval < 0.05:
    print("  ⟹  SIGNIFICANT (p < 0.05) — reject H₀.")
    print(f"     The Chancay opening is associated with a level shift of "
          f"{_coef:+.1f} units/month in import volume, after controlling for GDP.")
else:
    print("  ⟹  NOT significant (p ≥ 0.05) — fail to reject H₀.")
    print("     No statistically detectable level break at α = 0.05.")
    print("     Note: power is limited with only ~14 post-break observations.")
    print("     The break may materialise as more data accumulate post-2025.")
print()
print("  Full model summary (with dummy):")
print(results_chow.summary())

============================================================
  Structural Break Test — Chancay Megaport (Nov 2024)
============================================================
  SARIMAX order used : ARIMA(0, 1, 2) × Seasonal(0, 0, 1, 12)

  AIC without dummy  : 1019.81
  AIC with dummy     : 1018.02

  D_Chancay  coef = +33.3901
             s.e. = 13.8713
             t    = 2.4071
             p    = 0.0161

  ⟹  SIGNIFICANT (p < 0.05) — reject H₀.
     The Chancay opening is associated with a level shift of +33.4 units/month in import volume, after controlling for GDP.

In [89]:
# 5. PLOTTING THE FAN CHART WITH CHANCAY EVENT
plt.figure(figsize=(14, 7))

# Plot historical data
plt.plot(ts_data.index, ts_data['Import_Volume'], label='Historical (Up to Dec 2025)', color='black', linewidth=2)

colors = {'Optimistic (+5% GDP Growth)': '#2ca02c', 'Base Case (+3% GDP Growth)': '#1f77b4', 'Pessimistic (-2% GDP Contraction)': '#d62728'}
forecast_data = {}

for scenario_name, rate in growth_rates.items():
    monthly_rate = (1 + rate)**(1/12) - 1
    future_exo_values = [last_pbi * (1 + monthly_rate)**i for i in range(1, future_periods + 1)]
    future_exo = pd.DataFrame(future_exo_values, index=forecast_index, columns=['PBI_Construccion'])
    future_exo['PEN_USD'] = last_fx  # random-walk: FX stays at last observed

    forecast = results.get_forecast(steps=future_periods, exog=future_exo)
    mean_forecast = forecast.predicted_mean
    forecast_data[scenario_name] = mean_forecast

    plt.plot(forecast_index, mean_forecast, label=f'Forecast: {scenario_name}', color=colors[scenario_name], linewidth=2.5, marker='o', markersize=4)
    
    # --- New: Print Interval Summary ---
    ci = forecast.conf_int()
    print(f"\nForecast Interval for {scenario_name}:")
    print(f"  Final Month ({forecast_index[-1].strftime('%Y-%m')}):")
    print(f"  - Expected: {mean_forecast.iloc[-1]:,.2f}")
    print(f"  - 95% CI: [{ci.iloc[-1, 0]:,.2f} to {ci.iloc[-1, 1]:,.2f}]")
    if scenario_name == 'Base Case (+3% GDP Growth)':
        ci = forecast.conf_int()
        plt.fill_between(forecast_index, ci.iloc[:, 0], ci.iloc[:, 1], color=colors[scenario_name], alpha=0.15, label='95% Confidence Interval (Base)')

# --- NEW: ADDING THE CHANCAY PORT MARKER ---
chancay_date = pd.to_datetime('2024-11-01')
plt.axvline(x=chancay_date, color='purple', linestyle='-.', linewidth=2)
# We calculate a good Y-position so the text stays near the top of the chart dynamically
y_pos = plt.ylim()[0] + (plt.ylim()[1] - plt.ylim()[0]) * 0.75
plt.text(chancay_date + pd.DateOffset(days=15), y_pos, 'Chancay Megaport Inauguration',
         rotation=90, color='purple', fontweight='bold', fontsize=10)
# -------------------------------------------

# Chart Formatting
plt.title('2026 Used Machinery Import Volume Forecast (Accounting for Chancay Port Effect)', fontsize=14, pad=15)
plt.xlabel('Date')
plt.ylabel('Monthly Import Volume (Units)')
plt.legend(loc='upper left')
plt.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

# Forecast Start Line
plt.axvline(x=ts_data.index[-1], color='gray', linestyle='--', alpha=0.7)
plt.text(ts_data.index[-1] + pd.DateOffset(days=15), plt.ylim()[0], 'Forecast Start (Jan 2026)',
         rotation=90, verticalalignment='bottom', alpha=0.7)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('5_forecast.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

Forecast Interval for Optimistic (+5% GDP Growth):
  Final Month (2028-06):
  - Expected: 182.35
  - 95% CI: [70.60 to 294.09]

Forecast Interval for Base Case (+3% GDP Growth):
  Final Month (2028-06):
  - Expected: 180.26
  - 95% CI: [68.52 to 292.01]

Forecast Interval for Pessimistic (-2% GDP Contraction):
  Final Month (2028-06):
  - Expected: 175.32
  - 95% CI: [63.57 to 287.06]
In [91]:
# ── Johansen Cointegration Test — 3-Variable System ─────────────────────
# Variables ordered most-to-least exogenous: PEN_USD → PBI_Construccion → Import_Volume
# This ordering drives the Cholesky decomposition in the IRF (Sims, 1980).
# Prerequisite: all three series are I(1) (confirmed in stationarity cell).
# det_order=0 (restricted constant), k_ar_diff=1.
# Rank can now be 0, 1, or 2 for a 3-variable system.

from statsmodels.tsa.vector_ar.vecm import coint_johansen

_vecm_vars = ['PEN_USD', 'PBI_Construccion', 'Import_Volume']

jres = coint_johansen(ts_data[_vecm_vars], det_order=0, k_ar_diff=1)

print("--- Johansen Cointegration Test (3-variable system) ---")
print(f"{'Hypothesis':<18} {'Trace stat':>12} {'cv 90%':>10} {'cv 95%':>10} {'cv 99%':>10}  Decision")
print("-" * 72)
_rank = 0
for _r in range(len(jres.lr1)):
    _trace = jres.lr1[_r]
    _cv90, _cv95, _cv99 = jres.cvt[_r]
    _hyp   = f"H\u2080: rank \u2264 {_r}"
    _dec   = "REJECT \u2713" if _trace > _cv95 else "fail to reject"
    print(f"{_hyp:<18} {_trace:>12.4f} {_cv90:>10.4f} {_cv95:>10.4f} {_cv99:>10.4f}  {_dec}")
    if _trace > _cv95:
        _rank += 1

coint_rank_determined = max(_rank, 1)
print(f"\n\u27f9  Cointegration rank = {coint_rank_determined}"
      f"{'  (borderline \u2014 floored to 1)' if _rank == 0 else ''}")
print(f"   Variables in system: {_vecm_vars}")

--- Johansen Cointegration Test (3-variable system) ---
Hypothesis           Trace stat     cv 90%     cv 95%     cv 99%  Decision
------------------------------------------------------------------------
H₀: rank ≤ 0            31.8274    27.0669    29.7961    35.4628  REJECT ✓
H₀: rank ≤ 1             4.3576    13.4294    15.4943    19.9349  fail to reject
H₀: rank ≤ 2             0.8157     2.7055     3.8415     6.6349  fail to reject

⟹  Cointegration rank = 1
   Variables in system: ['PEN_USD', 'PBI_Construccion', 'Import_Volume']
In [93]:
# ── VECM: 3-Variable Error-Correction Model ──────────────────────────────
# Cholesky ordering: PEN_USD (most exogenous) → PBI_Construccion → Import_Volume
# All three confirmed I(1); cointegration rank from Johansen above.
# coint_rank_determined used — not hard-coded.

from statsmodels.tsa.vector_ar.vecm import VECM

_vecm_vars = ['PEN_USD', 'PBI_Construccion', 'Import_Volume']

vecm_model = VECM(ts_data[_vecm_vars],
                  k_ar_diff=1,
                  coint_rank=coint_rank_determined,
                  deterministic='co')
vecm_res = vecm_model.fit()
print(vecm_res.summary())

# ── 60-month (5-year) forecast → 2030 ────────────────────────────────────
forecast_vecm  = vecm_res.predict(steps=60)
forecast_dates = pd.date_range(
    ts_data.index[-1] + pd.offsets.MonthBegin(1), periods=60, freq='MS'
)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13, 5))
ax.plot(ts_data.index, ts_data['Import_Volume'],
        label='Historical', color='black', lw=2)
ax.plot(forecast_dates, forecast_vecm[:, 2],   # Import_Volume is index 2 in new ordering
        label='VECM Forecast (Import Volume)',
        color='steelblue', lw=2, marker='o', markersize=4, linestyle='--')
ax.axvline(ts_data.index[-1], color='gray', linestyle='--', alpha=0.6)
ax.set_title(
    f'VECM Forecast — Import Volume (to 2030)\n'
    f'System: {_vecm_vars}  |  rank={coint_rank_determined}',
    fontsize=12
)
ax.set_xlabel('Date'); ax.set_ylabel('Monthly Units')
ax.legend(); plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig('5_vecm_forecast.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()

# ── Error-correction interpretation ──────────────────────────────────────
print("\n--- Key Interpretation ---")
print("  alpha (loading coefficients) — speed of adjustment to long-run eq.:")
_alpha_df = pd.DataFrame(
    vecm_res.alpha,
    index=_vecm_vars,
    columns=[f'EC{i+1}' for i in range(vecm_res.alpha.shape[1])]
).round(4)
display(_alpha_df)
print("  beta (cointegrating vector) — long-run relationship:")
_beta_df = pd.DataFrame(
    vecm_res.beta,
    index=_vecm_vars,
    columns=[f'CV{i+1}' for i in range(vecm_res.beta.shape[1])]
).round(4)
display(_beta_df)

 Det. terms outside the coint. relation & lagged endog. parameters for equation PEN_USD

--- Key Interpretation ---
  alpha (loading coefficients) — speed of adjustment to long-run eq.:
EC1
PEN_USD 0.0037
PBI_Construccion 19.4909
Import_Volume 1.0651 
  beta (cointegrating vector) — long-run relationship:
CV1
PEN_USD 1.0000
PBI_Construccion -0.0303
Import_Volume 0.0146

Causal Direction of the Error-Correction Adjustment¶

The loading matrix (α) above identifies PBI_Construccion as the only variable that adjusts statistically significantly to deviations from the long-run equilibrium (α = 19.49, p < 0.001). Import_Volume (α = 1.07, p = 0.518) and PEN_USD (α = 0.004, p = 0.423) are weakly exogenous in the Engle–Granger sense: neither responds to disequilibrium shocks within the system. The economic interpretation is that Peru's construction sector adjusts its output level to absorb deviations between observed machinery imports and the long-run cointegrating relationship — not the reverse.

Economic coherence. This direction is consistent with the fiscal-investment execution channel that dominates short-run construction-sector dynamics in Peru: national infrastructure budgets and private capital-expenditure plans are approved on annual or multi-year horizons, but their physical execution depends on the availability of imported capital goods. When machinery imports surge ahead of trend — for example following the November 2024 Chancay megaport shock, which is associated with a +33.4 units/month break in import volume (Section Structural Break Test — Chancay Megaport) — the construction sector expands to deploy the newly available equipment, restoring the long-run equilibrium between input availability and output activity. The reverse causation (machinery imports adjusting to construction GDP shocks within the month) is inconsistent with the typical lead times for used commercial vehicle procurement, which span several months from order to customs clearance.

The error-correction direction is therefore both statistically identified and economically interpretable. The exchange rate PEN/USD enters the cointegrating vector with a normalised coefficient of 1.000 but does not itself adjust to disequilibrium (α ≈ 0): it operates as a slow-moving exogenous price determinant rather than as a within-system equilibrium-correction variable. This is consistent with Meese & Rogoff (1983) on short-horizon FX efficiency.

In [96]:
# ── Price VECM Out-of-Sample Evaluation on 2025 Holdout ──────────────────
from statsmodels.tsa.vector_ar.vecm import VECM, coint_johansen
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error, mean_absolute_error

# ── Lazy init: build price_ts if Cell 33 (main Price VECM) hasn't run yet ─
# price_ts is the 3-variable monthly panel (PEN_USD, PBI_Construccion, Avg_FOB_Price)
# defined in the main Price VECM cell below. This fallback lets the OOS evaluation
# run regardless of cell-execution order. df_gdp_monthly and df_fx come from Cell 5
# of the main panel construction.
if 'price_ts' not in dir():
    _price_monthly = (
        df_units
        .assign(Date=pd.to_datetime(
            df_units['ANO'].astype(str) + '-' + df_units['MES'].astype(str) + '-01'
        ))
        .groupby('Date')['US$ FOB']
        .median()
        .rename('Avg_FOB_Price')
    )
    _price_vars = ['PEN_USD', 'PBI_Construccion', 'Avg_FOB_Price']
    price_ts = (
        _price_monthly.to_frame()
        .join(df_gdp_monthly, how='inner')
        .join(df_fx,          how='left')
        .asfreq('MS')
        .dropna()
    )[_price_vars]
    price_ts = price_ts[price_ts.index <= pd.to_datetime('2025-12-31')]
    print(f'  [lazy init] price_ts built locally: {price_ts.shape}')

print('=' * 64)
print('  Price VECM — Out-of-Sample Holdout (2025)')
print('=' * 64)

# Train on 2015–2024
_p_train = price_ts[price_ts.index <= pd.to_datetime('2024-12-01')]
_p_test  = price_ts[(price_ts.index >  pd.to_datetime('2024-12-01')) &
                     (price_ts.index <= pd.to_datetime('2025-12-01'))]

print(f'  Training : {_p_train.index.min().date()} → {_p_train.index.max().date()}  (n={len(_p_train)})')
print(f'  Holdout  : {_p_test.index.min().date()} → {_p_test.index.max().date()}  (n={len(_p_test)})')

# Re-determine rank on training only
_jres_tr = coint_johansen(_p_train, det_order=0, k_ar_diff=1)
_rank_tr = sum(_jres_tr.lr1[i] > _jres_tr.cvt[i][1] for i in range(len(_jres_tr.lr1)))
_rank_tr = max(_rank_tr, 1)
print(f'  Train rank: {_rank_tr}')

# Fit VECM on training
_vecm_tr     = VECM(_p_train, k_ar_diff=1, coint_rank=_rank_tr, deterministic='co')
_vecm_tr_res = _vecm_tr.fit()

# Forecast 12 months
_fc_oos = _vecm_tr_res.predict(steps=len(_p_test))
_price_pred = _fc_oos[:, 2]   # Avg_FOB_Price column
_price_true = _p_test['Avg_FOB_Price'].values

# Metrics
_rmse_p = float(np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(_price_true, _price_pred)))
_mae_p  = float(mean_absolute_error(_price_true, _price_pred))
_mape_p = float(np.mean(np.abs((_price_true - _price_pred) / _price_true)) * 100)

print(f'\n  Holdout accuracy:')
print(f'    RMSE  = USD {_rmse_p:,.0f}')
print(f'    MAE   = USD {_mae_p:,.0f}')
print(f'    MAPE  = {_mape_p:.2f}%')
print(f'    Mean actual  2025 = USD {_price_true.mean():,.0f}')
print(f'    Mean forecast 2025 = USD {_price_pred.mean():,.0f}')
print(f'    Implied bias       = {(_price_pred.mean()/_price_true.mean() - 1)*100:+.1f}%')

# Plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 5))
_recent = price_ts[price_ts.index >= pd.to_datetime('2022-01-01')]
ax.plot(_recent.index, _recent['Avg_FOB_Price'], color='black', lw=1.2, label='Actual')
ax.plot(_p_test.index, _price_pred, color='steelblue', lw=2, marker='o', label='VECM forecast')
ax.scatter(_p_test.index, _price_true, color='crimson', s=30, zorder=3, label='Actual 2025')
ax.axvline(pd.to_datetime('2024-12-01'), color='gray', ls='--', lw=0.8)
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(plt.FuncFormatter(lambda v, _: f'${v:,.0f}'))
ax.set_title(f'Price VECM — 2025 Holdout (RMSE=${_rmse_p:,.0f}, MAE=${_mae_p:,.0f}, MAPE={_mape_p:.1f}%)')
ax.set_ylabel('Median FOB per shipment (USD)'); ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('5_sprice_vecm_2025_holdout.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

  [lazy init] price_ts built locally: (132, 3)
================================================================
  Price VECM — Out-of-Sample Holdout (2025)
================================================================
  Training : 2015-01-01 → 2024-12-01  (n=120)
  Holdout  : 2025-01-01 → 2025-12-01  (n=12)
  Train rank: 2

  Holdout accuracy:
    RMSE  = USD 12,479
    MAE   = USD 11,172
    MAPE  = 18.34%
    Mean actual  2025 = USD 58,703
    Mean forecast 2025 = USD 47,531
    Implied bias       = -19.0%
In [98]:
# Extract terminal values (index -1 is Dec 2030)
pen_start = ts_data['PEN_USD'].iloc[-1]
pen_end   = forecast_vecm[-1, 0]

pbi_start = ts_data['PBI_Construccion'].iloc[-1]
pbi_end   = forecast_vecm[-1, 1]

# 1. Calculate Annualized Growth Rate (CAGR) for Construction
# Horizon is 5 years (2026-2030)
pbi_annualized_growth = ((pbi_end / pbi_start)**(1/5) - 1) * 100

# 2. Determine PEN_USD trend
trend = "depreciates" if pen_end > pen_start else "appreciates"

print(f"Construction Annualized Growth: {pbi_annualized_growth:.2f}%")
print(f"PEN_USD Trend: {trend}")
print(f"PEN_USD End Value: {pen_end:.4f}")

Construction Annualized Growth: -3.34%
PEN_USD Trend: depreciates
PEN_USD End Value: 3.4617

Reduced-Form Impulse Response Functions — Specification Limitation¶

A 3-variable VAR(p) in levels (variables: PEN_USD, PBI_Construccion, Import_Volume) was initially fitted to derive reduced-form impulse response functions, following the standard treatment of I(1) cointegrated systems (Sims, Stock & Watson, 1990; Lütkepohl, 2005 §6.5). The multivariate Portmanteau (Hosking) whiteness test on the VAR residuals was applied across lag orders p ∈ {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8} at h = 12 and h = 24, and rejected the null hypothesis of residual white noise at p < 0.0001 at every tested lag order and horizon (see D3 in the Diagnostic Appendix). The persistent serial autocorrelation reflects the structural breaks documented in the SARIMAX analysis — particularly the Chancay megaport opening (November 2024), whose +33.4 units/month level shift cannot be accommodated by a constant-coefficient VAR specification.

Because residual whiteness is the prerequisite for valid IRF confidence band inference and Granger causality testing, the reduced-form IRF analysis is omitted from the dynamic narrative. The VECM cointegration result reported above (α_PBI = 19.49, p < 0.001; β established under Johansen rank = 1) is sufficient to characterise the long-run equilibrium dynamics among the three variables without invoking the misspecified VAR. A regime-switching or time-varying-parameter VAR specification capable of accommodating the post-2024 break is flagged as future work.

ARCH-LM Test — Validating Volatility Clustering Before GARCH¶

In [102]:
from statsmodels.stats.diagnostic import het_arch

# ARCH-LM test: does the variance cluster over time in the SARIMAX residuals?
# If p-value < 0.05 → ARCH effects exist → GARCH is justified
lm_stat, lm_pval, f_stat, f_pval = het_arch(results.resid, nlags=4)

print("--- ARCH-LM Test on SARIMAX Residuals ---")
print(f"LM Statistic : {lm_stat:.4f}")
print(f"LM p-value   : {lm_pval:.4f}  {'→ ARCH effects PRESENT — GARCH is justified' if lm_pval < 0.05 else '→ No ARCH effects — GARCH may not add value'}")

# Visual check: squared residuals autocorrelation
from statsmodels.graphics.tsaplots import plot_acf
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))
plot_acf(results.resid, ax=axes[0], lags=24, title='ACF of Residuals')
plot_acf(results.resid**2, ax=axes[1], lags=24, title='ACF of Squared Residuals (ARCH check)')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('5_acf_residuals.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

--- ARCH-LM Test on SARIMAX Residuals ---
LM Statistic : 20.6379
LM p-value   : 0.0004  → ARCH effects PRESENT — GARCH is justified
In [104]:
# GARCH(1,1) on SARIMAX residuals — model volatility clustering
from arch import arch_model

# We model the residuals (errors) of your SARIMAX model
residuals = results.resid
garch = arch_model(residuals, vol='Garch', p=1, q=1, dist='t')
garch_fit = garch.fit(disp='off')

print("\n--- GARCH Volatility Summary ---")
print(garch_fit.summary())

EXTRA for BUSINESS REPORT | Lead Scoring & Conclusions¶

In [107]:
# 1. Create a Lead Score based on your findings
# Three components (max 100 pts):
#   - Market Presence  (30 pts): normalised total FOB spend
#   - Wallet Gap       (40 pts): share NOT yet sourced from CPtrucks / Europe
#   - Growth Signal    (30 pts): flagged as Rising Star in clustering analysis
prospect_list = sow_final.copy()

prospect_list['is_rising_star'] = prospect_list.index.isin(success_stories.index)

prospect_list['Lead_Score'] = (
    (prospect_list['Total_Imported_FOB'] / prospect_list['Total_Imported_FOB'].max() * 30) +
    ((100 - prospect_list['Share_of_Wallet']) / 100 * 40) +
    (prospect_list['is_rising_star'].astype(int) * 30)
)

# 2. Add the "MBA Hook" (Which product to offer them)
prospect_list['Suggested_Offer'] = prospect_list['Main_Origin'].apply(
    lambda x: "Caterpillar Excavator" if x == "SUECIA" else "Volvo Loader"
)

# 3. Final ranking
belgian_hit_list = prospect_list.sort_values('Lead_Score', ascending=False)
display(belgian_hit_list[[
    'CLIENT_NAME', 'Share_of_Wallet', 'Total_Imported_FOB',
    'is_rising_star', 'Suggested_Offer', 'Lead_Score'
]].head(15))

In [109]:
# ── Blue Ocean Prospects: European Share of Wallet ────────────────────────
# Logic:
#   1. For every importer in df_units, compute total FOB and the share
#      sourced from European countries (proxy for CPtrucks-style suppliers).
#   2. Exclude known CPtrucks clients (sow_final index).
#   3. Flag companies with high total spend but low European sourcing —
#      these are the real Blue Ocean: big buyers who haven't tapped Europe yet.

_EUROPE = {
    'ALEMANIA',
    'AUSTRIA', 'BELGICA', 'BULGARIA', 'ESLOVENIA', 'ESPANA',
    'FINLANDIA', 'FRANCIA', 'IRLANDA {EIRE}', 'ITALIA', 'LITUANIA',
    'PAISES BAJOS', 'POLONIA', 'PORTUGAL', 'REINO UNIDO',
    'REPUBLICA CHECA', 'SUECIA', 'SUIZA',
}

_known_rucs = set(sow_final.index.astype(str))

# ── Per-importer aggregation ──────────────────────────────────────────────
_df = df_units.copy()
_df['RUC IMPORTADOR'] = _df['RUC IMPORTADOR'].astype(str)
_df['is_european']    = _df['PAIS DE ORIGEN'].isin(_EUROPE)

_agg = _df.groupby('RUC IMPORTADOR').agg(
    CLIENT_NAME   = ('IMPORTADOR',      'first'),
    Total_FOB     = ('US$ FOB',         'sum'),
    European_FOB  = ('US$ FOB',         lambda x: x[_df.loc[x.index, 'is_european']].sum()),
    Num_Shipments = ('US$ FOB',         'count'),
    Main_Origin   = ('PAIS DE ORIGEN',  lambda x: x.value_counts().index[0]),
    Main_Product  = ('PRODUCTO',        lambda x: x.value_counts().index[0]
                                        if x.notna().any() else 'UNKNOWN'),
    Department    = ('DEPARTAMENTO DE IMPORTADOR', lambda x: x.value_counts().index[0]),
).reset_index()

_agg['European_SOW_pct'] = (_agg['European_FOB'] / _agg['Total_FOB'] * 100).round(2)

# ── Filter: exclude CPtrucks clients, keep meaningful spend ───────────────
_min_fob = 1_000_000   # USD — at least $1M total machinery spend

blue_ocean = (
    _agg[
        (_agg['Total_FOB'] >= _min_fob)
    ]
    .sort_values(['European_SOW_pct', 'Total_FOB'], ascending=[True, False])
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)

blue_ocean.index += 1   # 1-based rank

print(f"Universe : {len(_agg):,} importers in df_units")
print(f"Blue Ocean candidates (FOB >= ${_min_fob:,}) : {len(blue_ocean)}")
print()
print("-- TOP 20 BLUE OCEAN PROSPECTS --")
print("   Ranked by lowest European SoW first, then highest total spend")
print()

_display_cols = ['RUC IMPORTADOR', 'CLIENT_NAME', 'Total_FOB', 'European_FOB',
                 'European_SOW_pct', 'Num_Shipments', 'Main_Origin',
                 'Main_Product', 'Department']

display(
    blue_ocean[_display_cols]
    .head(20)
    .style
    .format({'Total_FOB': '${:,.0f}', 'European_FOB': '${:,.0f}',
             'European_SOW_pct': '{:.1f}%'})
    .background_gradient(subset=['European_SOW_pct'], cmap='RdYlGn')
    .background_gradient(subset=['Total_FOB'], cmap='Blues')
)

# ── Export ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
blue_ocean[_display_cols].to_csv('CPTRUCKS_PROSPECTS_1M.csv', index_label='Rank')
print("Exported: CPTRUCKS_PROSPECTS_1M.csv")

Universe : 1,444 importers in df_units
Blue Ocean candidates (FOB >= $1,000,000) : 70

13 20516711559 BANCO SANTANDER PERU S.A. $1,032,420 $0 0.0% 2 ESTADOS UNIDOS PERFORADORA LIMA
14 20501523837 EXPLOMIN DEL PERU S.A. $1,023,880 $0 0.0% 6 CHILE MAQUINA PERFORADORA MONTADA SO LIMA
15 20100028698 FERREYROS SOCIEDAD AN?NIMA $35,773,932 $93,700 0.3% 169 BRASIL MOTONIVELADORA LIMA
16 20563254921 IMPORTACIONES DE MAQUINARIAS YARLEQUE S.A.C. $1,138,851 $34,000 3.0% 50 ESTADOS UNIDOS MINI CARGADOR UNKNOWN
17 20254813720 CHAVIN DE HUANTAR E.I.R.L $2,300,888 $73,243 3.2% 38 ESTADOS UNIDOS MINICARGADOR LIMA
18 20170072465 SOCIEDAD MINERA CERRO VERDE S.A.A. $2,439,603 $80,750 3.3% 3 ALEMANIA RODILLO VIBRADOR REMOLCADORNUM AREQUIPA
19 20302241598 KOMATSU-MITSUI MAQUINARIAS PERU S.A. $2,223,674 $84,241 3.8% 11 JAPON EXCAVADORA USADA LIMA
20 20479870595 CERAMICOS DEL NORTE EIRL $2,890,951 $113,000 3.9% 42 BRASIL CARGADOR FRONTAL LAMBAYEQUE 
Exported: CPTRUCKS_PROSPECTS_1M.csv

Per-Client & Prospect Forecast — Growth Trajectory & Model Profile¶

In [112]:
# ── Per-Client & Prospect Forecast: Growth Trajectory & Model Profile ─────
# For each top client (ranked by Lead Score) and top Blue Ocean prospect:
#   (a) Annual FOB history + linear trend + 2026 projection
#   (b) Top machinery models by shipment count
#   (c) Summary quick-action table for CPtrucks
#
# NOTE: Projections use OLS linear trend on annual FOB — a deliberate choice
# given sparse individual-client series (typically 3–8 annual obs). This is
# a trend extrapolation, not a structural forecast. Treat as directional.

from scipy.stats import linregress as _linreg

N_TOP = 5   # entities per group (clients / prospects)

# ── Identify top entities ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
_df_u = df_units.copy()
_df_u['RUC IMPORTADOR'] = _df_u['RUC IMPORTADOR'].astype(str)

_client_rucs  = belgian_hit_list.head(N_TOP).index.astype(str).tolist()
_client_names = belgian_hit_list.head(N_TOP)['CLIENT_NAME'].to_dict()
_client_names  = {str(k): v for k, v in _client_names.items()}

_prospect_rucs  = blue_ocean.head(N_TOP)['RUC IMPORTADOR'].astype(str).tolist()
_prospect_names = blue_ocean.head(N_TOP).set_index('RUC IMPORTADOR')['CLIENT_NAME'].to_dict()

# ── Annual FOB aggregation ────────────────────────────────────────────────
_ann = (
    _df_u.groupby(['RUC IMPORTADOR', 'ANO'])['US$ FOB']
    .sum().reset_index()
)

# ── Helper: linear trend + 2026 projection ───────────────────────────────
def _forecast(ruc):
    d = _ann[_ann['RUC IMPORTADOR'] == ruc].sort_values('ANO')
    if len(d) < 2:
        return None
    slope, intercept, r, _, _ = _linreg(d['ANO'], d['US$ FOB'])
    proj = max(intercept + slope * 2026, 0)
    fob0 = d['US$ FOB'].iloc[0]
    cagr = ((d['US$ FOB'].iloc[-1] / fob0) ** (1 / (len(d) - 1)) - 1) * 100 if fob0 > 0 else 0
    return dict(years=d['ANO'].values, fob=d['US$ FOB'].values,
                slope=slope, intercept=intercept,
                proj=proj, r2=r**2, cagr=cagr)

# ── Helper: top models ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def _top_models(ruc, n=4):
    sub = _df_u[_df_u['RUC IMPORTADOR'] == ruc].copy()
    sub['Label'] = (sub['MARCA_CLEAN'].fillna('?') + ' ' +
                    sub['MODELO'].fillna('')).str.strip()
    return (sub.groupby('Label')
               .agg(Count=('US$ FOB', 'count'), FOB=('US$ FOB', 'sum'))
               .sort_values('Count', ascending=False)
               .head(n).reset_index())

# ── Plot function ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def _plot_group(rucs, names, title, c_hist, c_proj):
    n = len(rucs)
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(n, 2, figsize=(17, 4 * n))
    if n == 1:
        axes = np.array([axes])
    fig.suptitle(title, fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', y=1.01)

    for i, ruc in enumerate(rucs):
        ax_l, ax_r = axes[i]
        name = names.get(ruc, ruc)
        fc   = _forecast(ruc)

        # ── Left: FOB trend + projection ─────────────────────────────────
        if fc is None:
            ax_l.text(0.5, 0.5, 'Insufficient data (<2 years)',
                      ha='center', va='center', transform=ax_l.transAxes)
            ax_l.set_title(name, fontsize=9)
        else:
            # Historical bars
            ax_l.bar(fc['years'], fc['fob'] / 1e3,
                     color=c_hist, alpha=0.8, zorder=2, label='Historical')
            # 2026 projection bar
            ax_l.bar(2026, fc['proj'] / 1e3,
                     color=c_proj, alpha=0.85, hatch='//',
                     edgecolor='black', lw=0.8, label='Projected 2026')
            # Trend line through historical + 2026
            _x  = np.linspace(fc['years'].min(), 2026.5, 200)
            _y  = (fc['slope'] * _x + fc['intercept']) / 1e3
            ax_l.plot(_x, _y, color='black', lw=1.4,
                      ls='--', alpha=0.55, label='OLS trend')

            _sign = '+' if fc['cagr'] >= 0 else ''
            ax_l.set_title(
                f"{name} "
                f"CAGR {_sign}{fc['cagr']:.1f}%  ·  "
                f"Proj. 2026: ${fc['proj']/1e3:,.0f}k  ·  "
                f"R²={fc['r2']:.2f}",
                fontsize=8.5
            )
            ax_l.set_ylabel('Annual FOB (USD thousands)')
            ax_l.yaxis.set_major_formatter(
                plt.FuncFormatter(lambda v, _: f'${v:,.0f}k'))
            ax_l.legend(fontsize=7.5)
            ax_l.set_xticks(list(fc['years']) + [2026])
            ax_l.tick_params(axis='x', rotation=45)

        # ── Right: Top models ─────────────────────────────────────────────
        mods = _top_models(ruc)
        if mods.empty:
            ax_r.text(0.5, 0.5, 'No model data',
                      ha='center', va='center', transform=ax_r.transAxes)
            ax_r.axis('off')
        else:
            _lbls = mods['Label'].values[::-1]
            _cnts = mods['Count'].values[::-1]
            _fobs = mods['FOB'].values[::-1]
            bars  = ax_r.barh(_lbls, _cnts, color=c_hist, alpha=0.8)
            ax_r.set_xlabel('Shipment count')
            ax_r.set_title('Top Machinery — Historical Purchases', fontsize=8.5)
            for j, (cnt, fob) in enumerate(zip(_cnts, _fobs)):
                ax_r.text(cnt + 0.15, j,
                          f'{cnt}x · ${fob/1e3:,.0f}k',
                          va='center', fontsize=8)
            ax_r.set_xlim(0, _cnts.max() * 1.45)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig('5_growth_project.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
    plt.show()

# ── Plot clients ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
_plot_group(_client_rucs, _client_names,
            'CPtrucks Active Clients — Growth Trajectory & Model Profile',
            c_hist='steelblue', c_proj='#2ca02c')

# ── Plot prospects ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
_plot_group(_prospect_rucs, _prospect_names,
            'Blue Ocean Prospects — Growth Trajectory & Model Profile',
            c_hist='#e07b39', c_proj='#d62728')

# ── Quick-action summary table ────────────────────────────────────────────
_rows = []
for _ruc, _nm, _grp in (
    [(r, _client_names.get(r, r),  'Client')   for r in _client_rucs] +
    [(r, _prospect_names.get(r, r),'Prospect') for r in _prospect_rucs]
):
    _fc   = _forecast(_ruc)
    _mods = _top_models(_ruc, n=3)
    _top3 = ' | '.join(_mods['Label'].tolist()) if not _mods.empty else 'N/A'
    _rows.append({
        'Type':             _grp,
        'Company':          _nm,
        'CAGR':             f"{_fc['cagr']:+.1f}%" if _fc else 'N/A',
        'Proj. 2026 FOB':   f"${_fc['proj']/1e3:,.0f}k" if _fc else 'N/A',
        'R²':               f"{_fc['r2']:.2f}"  if _fc else 'N/A',
        'Top 3 Models':     _top3,
    })

print("\n── QUICK-ACTION TABLE FOR CPTRUCKS ─────────────────────────────────")
_summary = pd.DataFrame(_rows).set_index('Company')
display(_summary.style.apply(
    lambda col: ['background-color: #d4edda' if v == 'Client'
                 else 'background-color: #fff3cd' for v in col],
    subset=['Type']
))

── QUICK-ACTION TABLE FOR CPTRUCKS ─────────────────────────────────
  Type CAGR Proj. 2026 FOB R² Top 3 Models
Company          
LARF BUSINESS E.I.R.L. Client +35.2% $955k 0.43 CATERPILLAR 329EL | VOLVO EC350EL | VOLVO L150H
JYC INTERNATIONAL BUSINESS S.A.C. Client -3.6% $515k 0.13 VOLVO L150H | CATERPILLAR 324EL | VOLVO L150F
ANKARA PERU E.I.R.L Client -46.7% $282k 1.00 CATERPILLAR 326FL | CATERPILLAR 323EL | CATERPILLAR 324 EL
FC MACHINERY & CONSTRUCTION E.I.R.L. Client +35.3% $523k 0.11 VOLVO L150H | UNKNOWN UNKNOWN | CATERPILLAR CS76
KARLA EQUIPMENT S.A.C. Client +5.0% $1,019k 0.06 CATERPILLAR 329EL | CATERPILLAR M322C | VOLVO EC380EL
MATERIALES Y SERVICIOS GENERALES FABRI E.I.R.L. Prospect +86.0% $3,050k 0.26 CATERPILLAR 320 | CATERPILLAR 330 | CATERPILLAR 323

BANCO DE CREDITO DEL PERU Prospect +15.0% $537k 0.02 CATERPILLAR 14M | CATERPILLAR 16M | DITCH WITCH JT4020

Machine Price Forecast — Top 5 Caterpillar & Volvo Models (2026)¶

In [114]:
# ── Machine Price Forecast: Top Caterpillar & Volvo Models ───────────────
# For each brand:
#   1. Select top 5 models by shipment count with ≥ 3 years of data
#   2. Build annual median FOB unit price series (median = robust to outliers)
#   3. Winsorise at 1st–99th percentile per model before aggregating
#   4. OLS linear trend → project 2026 price + 95% prediction interval
#   5. Two-panel figure: price trajectories (left) + 2026 bar comparison (right)
#   6. Summary table with price CAGR and projected 2026 price

from scipy.stats import linregress as _linreg, t as _t
import numpy as np

MIN_YEARS   = 3     # minimum distinct years required
N_MODELS    = 5     # models per brand
BRANDS      = {
    'CATERPILLAR': {'color': '#f4a261', 'accent': '#e76f51'},
    'VOLVO':       {'color': '#457b9d', 'accent': '#1d3557'},
}

# ── Helper: winsorise + annual median ────────────────────────────────────
def _annual_price(df, brand, model):
    sub = df[
        (df['MARCA_CLEAN'] == brand) &
        (df['MODELO'] == model) &
        (df['US$ FOB UNIT'] > 0)
    ]['US$ FOB UNIT'].copy()
    # Winsorise 1–99th percentile
    lo, hi = sub.quantile(0.01), sub.quantile(0.99)
    sub    = sub.clip(lo, hi)
    # Re-attach ANO and aggregate
    idx  = df.loc[sub.index, 'ANO']
    ann  = sub.groupby(idx).median()
    return ann.sort_index()

# ── Helper: OLS trend + prediction interval for 2026 ────────────────────
def _trend_forecast(ann_series):
    x = ann_series.index.values.astype(float)
    y = ann_series.values
    if len(x) < 2:
        return None
    slope, intercept, r, _, se = _linreg(x, y)
    proj    = intercept + slope * 2026
    # 95% PI: t_{n-2} × SE_pred
    n       = len(x)
    x_mean  = x.mean()
    ss_x    = ((x - x_mean) ** 2).sum()
    se_pred = se * np.sqrt(1 + 1/n + (2026 - x_mean)**2 / ss_x) if ss_x > 0 else se
    t_crit  = _t.ppf(0.975, df=max(n - 2, 1))
    margin  = t_crit * se_pred
    cagr    = ((y[-1] / y[0]) ** (1 / (n - 1)) - 1) * 100 if y[0] > 0 else 0
    return dict(slope=slope, intercept=intercept, proj=max(proj, 0),
                pi_lo=max(proj - margin, 0), pi_hi=proj + margin,
                r2=r**2, cagr=cagr, n=n)

# ── Select top models per brand ───────────────────────────────────────────
_df = df_units.copy()
_df['MODELO'] = _df['MODELO'].fillna('').str.strip()
_valid_mask   = (
    (_df['MODELO'] != '') &
    (~_df['MODELO'].str.upper().isin(['UNKNOWN'])) &
    (_df['US$ FOB UNIT'] > 0)
)

def _select_top(brand):
    sub  = _df[_valid_mask & (_df['MARCA_CLEAN'] == brand)]
    freq = (sub.groupby('MODELO')
               .agg(Count=('US$ FOB UNIT', 'count'),
                    Nyears=('ANO', lambda x: x.nunique()))
               .query(f'Nyears >= {MIN_YEARS}')
               .sort_values('Count', ascending=False)
               .head(N_MODELS))
    return freq.index.tolist()

# ── Main plot + table loop ────────────────────────────────────────────────
all_summary = []

for brand, style in BRANDS.items():
    models  = _select_top(brand)
    palette = plt.cm.tab10.colors

    fig, (ax_l, ax_r) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 5.5))
    fig.suptitle(f'{brand} — Top {N_MODELS} Model Price Forecast (Median FOB/unit, USD)',
                 fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')

    proj_vals, proj_lo, proj_hi, model_labels = [], [], [], []

    for idx, model in enumerate(models):
        ann  = _annual_price(_df, brand, model)
        fc   = _trend_forecast(ann)
        if fc is None:
            continue

        col  = palette[idx]
        xs   = ann.index.values
        ys   = ann.values / 1e3   # → USD thousands

        # ── Left: price trajectories ──────────────────────────────────
        ax_l.plot(xs, ys, marker='o', lw=2, color=col, label=model, zorder=3)
        # Trend line to 2026
        _xl  = np.linspace(xs.min(), 2026, 200)
        _yl  = (fc['intercept'] + fc['slope'] * _xl) / 1e3
        ax_l.plot(_xl, _yl, lw=1, ls='--', color=col, alpha=0.5)
        # 2026 projected point
        ax_l.scatter([2026], [fc['proj'] / 1e3], marker='D',
                     s=70, color=col, zorder=5)

        proj_vals.append(fc['proj'] / 1e3)
        proj_lo.append(fc['pi_lo'] / 1e3)
        proj_hi.append(fc['pi_hi'] / 1e3)
        model_labels.append(model)

        all_summary.append({
            'Brand':           brand,
            'Model':           model,
            'Obs (years)':     fc['n'],
            'Median Price 2025': f"${ann.iloc[-1]/1e3:,.1f}k" if ann.index[-1] == 2025 else 'N/A',
            'Price CAGR':      f"{fc['cagr']:+.1f}%",
            'Proj. 2026 (USD)': f"${fc['proj']:,.0f}",
            'PI 95%':          f"[${fc['pi_lo']:,.0f} – ${fc['pi_hi']:,.0f}]",
            'R²':              f"{fc['r2']:.2f}",
        })

    # ── Left panel formatting ──────────────────────────────────────────
    ax_l.axvline(2025.5, color='gray', ls=':', lw=1, alpha=0.7)
    ax_l.text(2025.6, ax_l.get_ylim()[1] * 0.97, 'forecast →',
              fontsize=8, color='gray', va='top')
    ax_l.set_xlabel('Year')
    ax_l.set_ylabel('Median FOB per unit (USD thousands)')
    ax_l.set_title('Annual Median Price — Historical & Trend', fontsize=10)
    ax_l.yaxis.set_major_formatter(
        plt.FuncFormatter(lambda v, _: f'${v:,.0f}k'))
    ax_l.legend(fontsize=9, loc='upper left')

    # ── Right panel: 2026 projected bar + PI whiskers ─────────────────
    _y_pos    = np.arange(len(model_labels))
    _bars     = ax_r.barh(_y_pos, proj_vals,
                           color=[palette[i] for i in range(len(model_labels))],
                           alpha=0.85, height=0.55)
    # Error bars (prediction interval)
    _err_lo   = [p - l for p, l in zip(proj_vals, proj_lo)]
    _err_hi   = [h - p for p, h in zip(proj_vals, proj_hi)]
    ax_r.errorbar(proj_vals, _y_pos,
                  xerr=[_err_lo, _err_hi],
                  fmt='none', color='black', capsize=5, lw=1.5)
    # Value labels
  # Change this section:
    for j, (val, lo_, hi_) in enumerate(zip(proj_vals, proj_lo, proj_hi)):
        ax_r.text(hi_ + 0.5, j,
                  f'${val:,.0f}k '  # Added a space here for readability
                  f'[${lo_:,.0f}k - ${hi_:,.0f}k]', # Ensure this line starts with f'
                  va='center', fontsize=7.5)

    ax_r.set_yticks(_y_pos)
    ax_r.set_yticklabels(model_labels, fontsize=10)
    ax_r.set_xlabel('Projected 2026 Median FOB per unit (USD thousands)')
    ax_r.set_title('2026 Price Projection with 95% Prediction Interval', fontsize=10)
    ax_r.xaxis.set_major_formatter(
        plt.FuncFormatter(lambda v, _: f'${v:,.0f}k'))
    ax_r.set_xlim(0, max(proj_hi) * 1.35)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig('5_price_forecast.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
    plt.show()

# ── Summary table ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n── PRICE FORECAST SUMMARY TABLE ────────────────────────────────────")
_df_summ = pd.DataFrame(all_summary).set_index(['Brand', 'Model'])
display(_df_summ.style.apply(
    lambda col: [
        'background-color: #fff3cd' if idx[0] == 'CATERPILLAR'
        else 'background-color: #d4edda'
        for idx in _df_summ.index
    ], axis=0
))

── PRICE FORECAST SUMMARY TABLE ────────────────────────────────────
    Obs (years) Median Price 2025 Price CAGR Proj. 2026 (USD) PI 95% R²
Brand Model            
CATERPILLAR 325BL 10 N/A +12.3% $58,322 [$56,146 – $60,498] 0.76
329EL 7 N/A +1.2% $78,451 [$73,500 – $83,402] 0.00
336 5 N/A -13.1% $104,798 [$50,111 – $159,484] 0.58
330FL 6 N/A -1.9% $87,812 [$83,047 – $92,578] 0.46
140K 9 N/A -19.9% $63,970 [$50,682 – $77,259] 0.47
VOLVO L150F 12 N/A +1.1% $59,639 [$57,571 – $61,707] 0.07
L150E 11 N/A -0.1% $48,899 [$47,220 – $50,578] 0.11
L150H 10 N/A -3.9% $92,901 [$86,989 – $98,814] 0.09
L120C 11 $30.4k +2.5% $34,821 [$34,222 – $35,421] 0.60
L150G 11 N/A -2.8% $66,184 [$63,763 – $68,606] 0.00
In [115]:
# Price Forecast: 3-Variable VECM (PEN_USD + PBI_Construccion + Avg_FOB_Price)
# ============================================================================
# Extends the volume VECM to the price dimension.
# Cholesky ordering (most -> least exogenous, consistent with main model):
#   PEN_USD -> PBI_Construccion -> Avg_FOB_Price
# Avg_FOB_Price = monthly MEDIAN unit FOB price (median is robust to outlier
# shipments; mean is pulled up by single large transactions).
# df_gdp_monthly and df_fx are reused from cell 5 (no CSV re-read).

from statsmodels.tsa.vector_ar.vecm import coint_johansen, VECM
from statsmodels.tsa.stattools import adfuller, kpss

# Build monthly median FOB price
_price_monthly = (
    df_units
    .assign(Date=pd.to_datetime(
        df_units['ANO'].astype(str) + '-' + df_units['MES'].astype(str) + '-01'
    ))
    .groupby('Date')['US$ FOB']
    .median()
    .rename('Avg_FOB_Price')
)

# Merge PBI_Construccion and PEN_USD (already built in cell 5)
_price_vars = ['PEN_USD', 'PBI_Construccion', 'Avg_FOB_Price'] #added avg_fob_price instead
price_ts = (
    _price_monthly.to_frame()
    .join(df_gdp_monthly, how='inner')
    .join(df_fx,          how='left')
    .asfreq('MS')
    .dropna()
)[_price_vars]
price_ts = price_ts[price_ts.index <= pd.to_datetime('2025-12-31')]

print(f'price_ts: {price_ts.shape}  '
      f'({price_ts.index[0].date()} -> {price_ts.index[-1].date()})')
print(price_ts.tail())

# Stationarity check on Avg_FOB_Price
# (PBI_Construccion and PEN_USD already confirmed I(1) in cell 8)
_adf_lv  = adfuller(price_ts['Avg_FOB_Price'].dropna(), autolag='AIC')
_adf_d1  = adfuller(price_ts['Avg_FOB_Price'].diff().dropna(), autolag='AIC')
_kpss_lv = kpss(price_ts['Avg_FOB_Price'].dropna(), regression='c', nlags='auto')
_kpss_d1 = kpss(price_ts['Avg_FOB_Price'].diff().dropna(), regression='c', nlags='auto')

print('\nAvg_FOB_Price stationarity:')
print(f'  Level      ADF p={_adf_lv[1]:.4f}  KPSS p={_kpss_lv[1]:.4f}'
      f'  -> {"non-stationary" if _adf_lv[1] >= 0.05 else "stationary"}')
print(f'  1st diff   ADF p={_adf_d1[1]:.4f}  KPSS p={_kpss_d1[1]:.4f}'
      f'  -> {"stationary" if _adf_d1[1] < 0.05 else "still non-stationary"}')
_price_I1 = (_adf_lv[1] >= 0.05) and (_adf_d1[1] < 0.05)
print(f'  => {"I(1) confirmed -- eligible for Johansen" if _price_I1 else "AMBIGUOUS -- interpret with caution"}')

# Johansen cointegration test (3-variable system)
print('\n--- Johansen Test: PEN_USD + PBI_Construccion + Avg_FOB_Price ---')
jres_p = coint_johansen(price_ts, det_order=0, k_ar_diff=1)
print(f'{"Hypothesis":<18} {"Trace":>10} {"cv 90%":>10} {"cv 95%":>10}  Decision')
print('-' * 56)
_price_rank = 0
for _r in range(len(jres_p.lr1)):
    _tr  = jres_p.lr1[_r]
    _cv90, _cv95, _ = jres_p.cvt[_r]
    _dec = 'REJECT \u2713' if _tr > _cv95 else 'fail to reject'
    print(f'H\u2080: rank \u2264 {_r}  {_tr:>10.4f} {_cv90:>10.4f} {_cv95:>10.4f}  {_dec}')
    if _tr > _cv95:
        _price_rank += 1
_price_coint_rank = max(_price_rank, 1)
print(f'\n\u27f9  Cointegration rank = {_price_coint_rank}'
      f'{" (borderline -- floored to 1)" if _price_rank == 0 else ""}')

# VECM fit
vecm_p     = VECM(price_ts, k_ar_diff=1,
                  coint_rank=_price_coint_rank, deterministic='co')
vecm_p_res = vecm_p.fit()
print(vecm_p_res.summary())

# 60-month (5-year) price forecast -> 2030
_pfcast     = vecm_p_res.predict(steps=60)
_pfcast_idx = pd.date_range(
    price_ts.index[-1] + pd.offsets.MonthBegin(1), periods=60, freq='MS'
)
# _pfcast columns: [0]=PEN_USD, [1]=PBI_Construccion, [2]=Avg_FOB_Price

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(15, 5))

# Left panel: historical + VECM price forecast
ax = axes[0]
ax.plot(price_ts.index, price_ts['Avg_FOB_Price'],
        color='black', lw=2, label='Historical (median FOB)')
ax.plot(_pfcast_idx, _pfcast[:, 2],
        color='steelblue', lw=2, linestyle='--',
        marker='o', markersize=3, label='VECM Forecast')
ax.axvline(price_ts.index[-1], color='gray', linestyle='--', alpha=0.6)
ax.set_title('Avg FOB Price Forecast (3-variable VECM, 2026-2030)',
             fontsize=11)
ax.set_ylabel('USD per shipment (monthly median)')
ax.set_xlabel('Date')
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(
    __import__('matplotlib').ticker.FuncFormatter(
        lambda v, _: f'${v:,.0f}'))
ax.legend()

# Right panel: PEN/USD trajectory driving the price forecast
ax2 = axes[1]
ax2.plot(price_ts.index, price_ts['PEN_USD'],
         color='#e07b39', lw=1.8, label='PEN/USD (historical)')
ax2.plot(_pfcast_idx, _pfcast[:, 0],
         color='#e07b39', lw=1.8, linestyle='--',
         label='PEN/USD (VECM path)')
ax2t = ax2.twinx()
ax2t.plot(price_ts.index, price_ts['PBI_Construccion'],
          color='#457b9d', lw=1.8, label='PBI Construccion (hist)')
ax2t.plot(_pfcast_idx, _pfcast[:, 1],
          color='#457b9d', lw=1.8, linestyle='--',
          label='PBI Construccion (VECM path)')
ax2.set_ylabel('PEN / USD', color='#e07b39')
ax2t.set_ylabel('PBI Construccion Index', color='#457b9d')
ax2.set_title('Exogenous Variable Paths (VECM system)', fontsize=11)
_lines = ax2.get_lines() + ax2t.get_lines()
ax2.legend(_lines, [l.get_label() for l in _lines],
           loc='upper left', fontsize=8)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('5_fob_forecast.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

# Long-run relationship and adjustment speed
print('\n--- Long-run Cointegrating Vector (beta) ---')
_beta_p = pd.DataFrame(
    vecm_p_res.beta,
    index=_price_vars,
    columns=[f'CV{i+1}' for i in range(vecm_p_res.beta.shape[1])]
).round(4)
display(_beta_p)

print('\n--- Speed of Adjustment (alpha) ---')
_alpha_p = pd.DataFrame(
    vecm_p_res.alpha,
    index=_price_vars,
    columns=[f'EC{i+1}' for i in range(vecm_p_res.alpha.shape[1])]
).round(4)
display(_alpha_p)

_proj_2026 = _pfcast[11, 2]   # month 12 = Dec 2026
_last_price = price_ts['Avg_FOB_Price'].iloc[-1]
print(f'\n2026 price projection (VECM, Dec-2026) : USD {_proj_2026:,.0f} per shipment')
print(f'Current price (last observation)        : USD {_last_price:,.0f} per shipment')
print(f'Implied change                           : {(_proj_2026/_last_price - 1)*100:+.1f}%')

price_ts: (132, 3)  (2015-01-01 -> 2025-12-01)
             PEN_USD  PBI_Construccion  Avg_FOB_Price
Date                                                 
2025-08-01  3.542385        233.360658       51366.00
2025-09-01  3.501805        251.295255       51300.00
2025-10-01  3.413227        268.988237       66496.75
2025-11-01  3.372630        247.335666       62000.00
2025-12-01  3.366863        353.050702       59294.00

Avg_FOB_Price stationarity:
  Level      ADF p=0.0162  KPSS p=0.0100  -> stationary
  1st diff   ADF p=0.0000  KPSS p=0.1000  -> stationary
  => AMBIGUOUS -- interpret with caution

--- Johansen Test: PEN_USD + PBI_Construccion + Avg_FOB_Price ---
Hypothesis              Trace     cv 90%     cv 95%  Decision
--------------------------------------------------------
H₀: rank ≤ 0     55.2619    27.0669    29.7961  REJECT ✓
H₀: rank ≤ 1     23.1827    13.4294    15.4943  REJECT ✓
H₀: rank ≤ 2      3.1567     2.7055     3.8415  fail to reject
